hazard.summary: Batch download hits 429 and exhausts retries because metadata calls occur before skip-check and Retry-After pacing is not honored.
signals: Max Retries, reason: too many 429 error responses

constraints: 429 must be treated as a pacing signal and honor Retry-After when present.
Skip/existence check must occur before upstream metadata fetch.
Retries must have a bounded envelope (max_elapsed or max_attempts).
Concurrency must be capped relative to upstream quota.
verification: Reproduce large playlist batch and observe 429 failure.
Enable Retry-After honoring + exponential backoff and confirm system slows instead of crashing.
Move skip-check before metadata reinit and confirm restart does not re-trigger upstream calls.
Measure requests/minute to confirm rate stays within upstream quota.
notes: Root cause discussion quantifies request volume and identifies metadata reinit ordering bug. Concurrency reduction mitigates but does not solve pacing correctness.
